data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-04T00:53:37.155Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known EF Core change with the Cosmos DB provider. Let me explain what I know:

In EF Core, the Azure Cosmos DB provider has been moving toward async-only operations. In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider were deprecated but could still work via an opt-in flag. In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), the sync-over-async support would be fully removed.


The underlying issue is that the Azure Cosmos DB SDK is inherently async-only, so synchronous LINQ operations were always implemented as sync-over-async (blocking on async calls), which introduces deadlock risks. EF Core 9 started warning about this pattern, and by .NET 11, that workaround is being removed entirely, forcing developers to migrate their code to async patterns. sync-over-async support has been progressively deprecated. In earlier versions, you could opt in via `EnableSyncOverAsync` or similar configuration, but the trajectory is toward removing sync support entirely for the Cosmos provider.

Given the user's specific date (April 2026) and the .NET 10 → .NET 11 migration scenario, .NET 10 would be released November 2025 and .NET 11 would be November 2026. In EF Core 9 with .NET 9, the Cosmos DB provider started throwing `InvalidOperationException` for synchronous operations by default, though you could opt in to sync-over-async behavior. By .NET 11/EF Core 11, that opt-in would likely be completely removed, forcing all synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()` on Cosmos to throw exceptions with no workaround except converting to async equivalents.
